summary: IMPL_REVIEW multi-vendor convergence (claude_code + codex + gemini, 3 rounds, min_quorum=2, fix_mode=inline with no-op fix_callback) exhausted max_rounds without converging. 4 cross-vendor-confirmed CRITICAL blockers remain, all describing real defects in the shipped UI/backend integration that IMPL_ITERATE's quality gates did not catch (backend pytest passes, frontend unit tests pass — none exercise App-level composition or multi-change integration). Escalating to operator for scope decision: apply inline fixes (~4 file groups), spawn implementer Agent, or accept-and-ship with documented carve-outs.

rounds.notes: Initial multi-vendor pass. All 3 vendors participated; quorum 3/3.

fix_callback was intentional no-op (driver design choice: package_authors all claude → targeted dispatch would self-loop). Findings repeated and several promoted unconfirmed→confirmed (cross-vendor agreement).

Consensus synthesizer's relaxed final-round semantics demoted unconfirmed findings, leaving 4 hard-confirmed blockers. claude_code 20 raw findings, codex 6, gemini 6. All cross-vendor matches scored 0.95 (very high).

blocking_findings_remaining.description: App.tsx (line 26) still renders only `<Board issues={issues} />`. The SyncPointBanner component is implemented and tested but never mounted — flagged in Round 1 (codex #1) and Round 2 (all three vendors); third round, still unaddressed. Proposal §3 names the banner as one of three MVP surfaces; the feature is effectively missing from the shipped UI.
blocking_findings_remaining.recommended_fix: In apps/kanban-viz/src/App.tsx, render `<SyncPointBanner apiUrl={API_URL} apiKey={API_KEY} />` above `<Board>`; thread API_URL/API_KEY through.

blocking_findings_remaining.description: Card.tsx renders title, change_id, assignee, and timestamp only — it does not import VendorSwimlanes and the in-flight branch is absent. Flagged in Round 1 (codex #2, claude #2) and Round 2 (claude #2, codex #2), still unaddressed. Vendor swimlanes are MVP surface #2 per proposal §3.
blocking_findings_remaining.recommended_fix: When issue.status ∈ {claimed, running}, render `<VendorSwimlanes agents={agentsForIssue} completed={false} />`; project per-issue agent activity through useCoordinator and pass it as a prop or via a context.

blocking_findings_remaining.description: useCoordinator.fetchIssues (line 47-58) sends `labels: changeIds.map(id => 'change:'+id)` as a single array. IssueService.list_issues treats label arrays as ALL-of (AND), so multi-change boards return the intersection (almost always empty) instead of the union. Identical bug in agent-coordinator/src/event_stream.py:170 `_build_snapshot` (`labels=[f'change:{cid}' for cid in change_ids]`).
blocking_findings_remaining.recommended_fix: Iterate change_ids and merge results (Promise.all + dedupe by id) on the frontend; same fix in `_build_snapshot`; add a regression test for ≥2 change_ids.

blocking_findings_remaining.description: Kick flow remains broken end-to-end. sync_points.get_sync_points_status (lines 106-112) emits blocker rows with only `agent_id` and `last_heartbeat_iso` — no `change_id`. SyncPointBanner.tsx line 208 then passes the literal string "unknown" to handleKickClick, which feeds it into the `change_id` body parameter of POST /agents/{agent_id}/kick. The kick endpoint runs `worktree.py teardown unknown --agent-id <agent_id> --force` (coordination_api.py:2885-2889) — that change_id will never match a real registry entry, so registry_cleared stays False, kicked=False, and the banner button is non-functional.
blocking_findings_remaining.recommended_fix: (a) include `change_id` in each blocker dict in sync_points.py (registry entries carry it); (b) extend SyncPointStatus + blocker types to surface change_id; (c) thread change_id through SyncPointBanner.handleKickClick instead of hardcoding 'unknown'; (d) integration test that drives a real blocker and asserts non-default change_id reaches the registry teardown.

demoted_unconfirmed_findings_note: 22 additional unconfirmed findings exist in .review-cache/round-3/findings-*.json (mostly single-vendor flags from claude_code's higher-recall pass: 20 vs codex 6 vs gemini 6). These include schema-validation gaps for file-write helpers, /audit vs /audit/v2 spec interpretation, sse-starlette missing from pyproject.toml [api], React setIssues nested-update anti-pattern, kick_agent --agent-id null handling, and saveView/drag-to-Ready/force-release-lock UI surfaces marked complete but absent. The consensus synthesizer's final-round semantics demoted these to non-blocking; operator should still skim them before VALIDATE.

wp-saved-views.notes: saveView/drag-to-Ready/force-release-lock features marked complete in tasks but vendors flag them as absent; demoted in final round but operator should verify.

claude_code.notes: Primary vendor on all 4 confirmed blockers. Highest recall.

codex.notes: Confirmed F1, F2, F3. Higher precision than claude_code.

convergence_loop_config.post_fix_validator: ruff check on critical source files

escalation_reason: max_rounds reached with 4 confirmed cross-vendor blockers. Operator decision required: (A) apply inline fixes for F1/F2/F3/F5 (~6-8 files, ~2-4h work, ships fully spec-compliant MVP), (B) spawn fresh implementer Agent with the 4 fix-specs, (C) accept partial MVP with documented carve-outs (App.tsx wiring + multi-change semantics are not optional per proposal §3 so this path is not recommended), (D) abandon.
operator_recommendation: Option A (orchestrator applies inline fixes). The 4 fixes are well-scoped with vendor-supplied file:line references. Estimated diff: ~150 LOC across 6 files + 1 new integration test. After fixes land, re-run IMPL_REVIEW with max_rounds=1 to confirm clean (since the same vendors will re-evaluate the same code, this is a verification round, not a convergence round; the relaxed final-round semantics are acceptable here because we're confirming a known-good state).
